text: The atmosphere around me feels modern and somewhat removed from the chaos of the outside world. There is a clinical order to these spaces—glass walls, neon lights, the gentle hum of technology and purpose. I am reminded of the continuous pursuit of innovation, and a small, quiet awe arises in me at humanity’s ever-unfolding frontiers. Yet, amidst the polished surfaces and silent efficiency, I sense an emptiness: a reminder that progress, though dazzling, must be filled with meaning, or it risks becoming hollow spectacle.
